'''Jacob Fairbank''' (born 4 March 1990) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays {{rlp|pr|sr}} and {{rlp|lf}} for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Fairbank was born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire]], England.

He is the nephew of [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] legend [[Karl Fairbank]].

==Huddersfield==
Fairbank signed for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]], and progressed through the youth system, finally making his début in Round 23 of the 2011 season against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. Since then he made a number of appearances for the first team. He also spent time on loan at [[Oldham RLFC]].

==Bradford==
In 2013, he was offered the chance to go to another [[Super League]] side on loan, [[Bradford Bulls]]. Fairbank signed a one-month deal at Bradford after they suffered an injury crisis in their pack. Fairbank featured in Round 15 ([[Salford Red Devils|Salford]]) to Round 16 ([[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]). He was injured for Round 17-18.

==Wakefield Trinity Wildcats==
On 1 May 2014, Fairbank signed for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]  on a one-month loan deal.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12214/9291512/super-league-huddersfield-giants-loan-jacob-fairbank-to-wakefield-wildcats |title=Super League: Huddersfield Giants loan Jacob Fairbank to Wakefield Wildcats | Huddersfield Giants Rugby News, Fixtures, Results | Sky Sports |access-date=2 May 2014 |archive-date=1 May 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140501214825/http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12214/9291512/super-league-huddersfield-giants-loan-jacob-fairbank-to-wakefield-wildcats |url-status=dead }}</ref>

==Halifax==
In 2015, Fairbank signed a contract to join [[Halifax Panthers|Halifax]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/halifax-egg-man-jacob-fairbank-aiming-crack-it-against-st-helens-1752606/|title=Halifax egg man Jacob Fairbank aiming to crack it against St Helens|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk|date=23 July 2019 }}</ref>
